Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 11:02 ET
Massive SPY gamma unwind ahead of NFP creates dangerous amplification setup
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. SPY negative gamma at $-3.9B creates 4x normal volatility amplification into tomorrow's NFP print
  • 2. Index puts dominate while single-names show mixed flow — classic dispersion hedge ahead of macro data
  • 3. GOOG call block and tech death crosses signal sector rotation as institutions exit mega-cap tech
NARRATIVE — negative gamma NFP trap

Dealers sit on $-3.9B of negative SPY gamma with the gamma flip at $661 — just $5 above current levels — creating extreme vulnerability into tomorrow's NFP release. This negative gamma regime means any directional move will be amplified 3-4x as dealers mechanically hedge in the same direction, with VIX at 23.9 already pricing elevated uncertainty. The index P/C ratio of 1.53 versus single-name 1.03 reveals institutions buying index protection while maintaining selective single-name exposure — a textbook dispersion play ahead of macro volatility. The most striking divergence appears in mega-cap tech where death crosses in MSFT, META, and NFLX contradict their strong 13F builds from Q4. Millennium's $2.5B MSFT add and massive AMZN reversal now face technical breakdown signals, suggesting either poor timing or contrarian positioning ahead of earnings season. Meanwhile, the 5,500-lot GOOG May 310C block at $5.40 targets a 15% upside move through earnings, directly opposing Druckenmiller's complete META exit and broader tech pessimism. Vol surface tensions amplify these cross-currents with 14 names in backwardation including AAPL and AMZN, while implied correlation at 0.20 signals maximum stock-specific opportunity. The combination of cheap single-name vol, rich index vol, and negative dealer gamma creates ideal conditions for dispersion trades — but only if tomorrow's NFP doesn't trigger the systematic unwind that current positioning suggests.

Layer 1: Sentiment -33Layer 2: GEX -$3.9BLayer 9: NFP tomorrow
